findings.problem: The prose leans on the same inferential comparison scaffold far past the twice-is-coincidence threshold: key descriptive beats keep arriving through repeated "as if" and "like" turns, so sentence texture starts to feel mechanically familiar instead of freshly pressured.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated comparison beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:137, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:221. Keep only the strongest one or two similes; convert the rest into direct sensory fact, plain physical action, or Nami's blunt judgment so the prose stops explaining pressure through the same comparison shape.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:137, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:221 contain no more than two inferential "as if"/"like" comparison scaffolds total, and at least four of those beats land as direct description or action; FAIL if the same sentence pattern still repeats through the caregiving and dawn setup.

findings.problem: The fever-dream cluster drops into verbless image fragments ("Jun's drowned infant face..." / "Sereya's ferry pole...") that read as clipped lyric shorthand. That violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration and weakens what should be fully rendered fever perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the dream sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:103 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:104 into grammatically complete clauses anchored to Nami's body and perception. Keep the dream logic, but give each image an explicit action or state instead of presenting it as a standalone noun-phrase fragment.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if every sentence in the revised cluster at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:103 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:104 contains an explicit verb while the fever pressure remains legible on the page; FAIL if any standalone image fragment without a governing verb remains in that passage.

summary: This chapter clears its structural and continuity obligations: it keeps the promised caregiving aftermath, lands the Riva/Lale reckoning, gets Daro's childhood river-hearing confession on the page, and moves the family onto the slower hand-poled mission without any MEDIUM+ continuity or dialogue-register failures. It still fails review on prose because the sentence-making falls into a repeated "as if"/"like" comparison tic and a clipped fever-fragment patch that breaks the constitution's narration rule.
